basicDetails.implementingAgency: Uttarakhand Science Education And Research Centre

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme “Development and Dissemination of e-Content” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Science Education and Research Centre (USERC) under the Department of Science and Technology, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide free, high-quality e-learning materials to students of classes 9 to 12, covering subjects such as Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, English, etc. in collaboration with reputed ed-tech platforms.
schemeContent.benefits_md: The high-quality e-learning materials from online education providers (platforms such as Byju's and Vedantu) are made available to students of classes 9 to 12 through free bilingual online and offline lectures in Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, and English.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to provide free, high-quality e-learning materials to students of classes 9 to 12, covering subjects such as Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, English, etc. in collaboration with reputed ed-tech platforms.

children.text: The scheme “Development and Dissemination of e-Content” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Science Education and Research Centre (USERC) under the Department of Science and Technology, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide free, high-quality e-learning materials to students of classes 9 to 12, covering subjects such as Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, English, etc. in collaboration with reputed ed-tech platforms.

The high-quality e-learning materials from online education providers (platforms such as Byju's and Vedantu) are made available to students of classes 9 to 12 through free bilingual online and offline lectures in Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, and English.
Students can access certain e-learning materials by visiting the USERC website,
. However, to access the full course, they can download the Vidyasaar app from the Google Play Store and register their mobile number free of charge.
In schools without internet access, USERC provides a device to the school, enabling students to access the entire course offline.

applicationProcess.process_md: - Students can access certain e-learning materials by visiting the USERC website, [https://www.userc.in](https://www.userc.in). However, to access the full course, they can download the Vidyasaar app from the Google Play Store and register their mobile number free of charge. - In schools without internet access, USERC provides a device to the school, enabling students to access the entire course offline.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The applicant must be a student from Uttarakhand.

children.text: The applicant must be a student from Uttarakhand.
